title: Fluid Wave, :has() Grid, Neon Flicker, Rubber Band, Expand Cards
description: Five fresh UI patterns: SVG fluid wave morph, CSS :has() sibling reactor, neon sign flicker, rubber band elastic drag, and animated expandable cards with interpolate-size.

drops.title: SVG Fluid Wave Morph
drops.description: Three layered SVG wave paths animated by morphing bezier control points in real-time, with presets and live sliders.
drops.concept: buildWavePath() computes 80 sample points using summed sines at different frequencies, constructing an SVG path string each rAF frame. Three waves with different phases and amplitudes layer to create fluid depth.
drops.source: SVG wave morphing techniques — Chris Gannon / SVG path animation patterns

drops.title: CSS :has() Sibling Reactor
drops.description: An emoji grid where hovered cells light up while siblings dim — driven by :has() with zero JavaScript. Plus a form validation indicator using :has(input:valid).
drops.concept: .reactor-grid:has(.r-cell:hover) .r-cell:not(:hover) selects all non-hovered cells whenever any sibling is hovered — true CSS parent/sibling awareness. The form demo uses :has(input:valid:not(:placeholder-shown)) to colour labels green or red.
drops.source: CSS :has() selector — MDN Web Docs, Bramus Van Damme

interaction.mouse: Hover cells; type in form fields
interaction.touch: Tap cells; type in form fields

drops.title: Neon Sign Flicker
drops.description: Five neon signs with per-letter staggered flicker animations. Click to kill tubes, hover to buzz, or trigger random faults and full blackouts.
drops.concept: Each letter is wrapped in a <span> with a randomised animation-duration and animation-delay, so flicker timing is naturally unsynchronised. text-shadow layers at 4 radii (4px→56px) simulate neon glow. Dead letters use color:rgba override and animation:none.
drops.source: CSS text-shadow neon effects — CSS-Tricks, CodePen community

interaction.mouse: Click letters; hover to buzz; use buttons

drops.title: Rubber Band Elastic Drag
drops.description: Six draggable cards stretch up to a clamped max distance, then spring back with configurable stiffness, damping, and overshoot.
drops.concept: getBoundingClientRect() stores each item's resting origin. During drag the displacement is clamped to maxStr. On release a spring loop applies force = -stiffness * x each frame, multiplied by damping, until velocity and position fall below 0.1px. A dashed SVG line shows the rubber-band tether.
drops.source: Rubber band / elastic drag spring physics — Josh Comeau, native iOS scroll behaviour

drops.title: Expandable Cards — interpolate-size
drops.description: An accordion of four cards that expand and collapse with a smooth height:0 → height:auto CSS transition — no JavaScript height measurement.
drops.concept: :root { interpolate-size: allow-keywords } enables the browser to interpolate between a definite length and an intrinsic keyword like auto. The card body simply transitions height:0 → height:auto. A max-height fallback is applied for unsupporting browsers, detected via CSS.supports().
drops.source: CSS interpolate-size spec — MDN Web Docs, Chrome Status
